text_snippet: Recently, the research on metal three-dimensional lattice structure mainly focuses on body-centered cubic (BCC) and other topologies derived from it.
AutoCAD software was used to design the lattice structures in cubes of dimensions 25 × 25 × 25 mm so that they could be built in one batch to reduce production deviation.
All of the lattice structures were made from a 316L stainless steel powder with an average particle size of 35 ± 10 µm.
SLM technique as the additive manufacturing process was used to fabricate lattice structures.

Table 2 The parameters of design configuration for lattice structure. Lattice structure D (mm) P (%) 1 1 12.60 12.60 Sample designation C1 C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 D1 D2 D3 D4 D5 D6 D7 D8 LED (J/mm 3 ) 109.7 89.29 71.43 71.23 49.02 39.22 29.41 23.81

Uni-axial compression and tensile tests were carried out to assess the mechanical properties and energy absorption of the lattice structures by using a universal testing machine. During the compression tests, the lattice structures were centrally located between two plates. The speed of loading was set a constant of 6 mm/min for all of the tests.
